##NAMES=Avogadrite
##RRUFFID=R110062
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=KBF_4_
##LOCALITY=Mount Vesuvius, near Naples, Campania, Italy
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=James Shigley
##DESCRIPTION=Colorless consolidated granular. Some of the material consists of transparent gemmy crystals. The unidentified associated yellow material is a thin coating, something like a bubble surface, that turns white under acetone.
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R110062
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 8.671 b: 5.488 c: 7.0348 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 334.8 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
